skill.name: docusign-automation
skill.description: Guides DocuSign template, envelope, signing, and status workflows through the Rube MCP DocuSign toolkit.
skill.summary: Automate DocuSign templates, envelopes, signing, and status checks through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found that the external command and weak cryptography alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, DocuSign terminology, and GUID examples. The only confirmed risk factor is use of the external Rube MCP endpoint for DocuSign automation, which is expected for this skill and should be disclosed to users.

low_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to configure the Rube MCP endpoint for DocuSign workflows. This is expected functionality, but it creates a third-party network and OAuth trust boundary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 20 contains a real external MCP URL. The surrounding setup text shows legitimate service configuration rather than hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution alerts are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and MCP tool identifiers. No executable script, shell command, or user-controlled command construction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation for MCP tool names and workflow examples. The skill contains no source code that invokes a shell.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match DocuSign wording, signature workflow terms, or GUID examples. No cryptographic API, hash function call, or encryption implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content describes DocuSign envelopes and status fields. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Dismissed
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert maps to guidance about validating recipient email addresses before sending envelopes. No host, account, process, or environment discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 129 is a business workflow prerequisite for DocuSign recipients. It does not request system information or runtime environment details.

content.value_statement: DocuSign workflows often require careful template, recipient, and envelope handling. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP actions for repeatable signing workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists available DocuSign templates through the DocuSign toolkit.
Retrieves template details, roles, fields, and identifiers before use.
Creates envelopes from templates with recipient role assignments.
Sends draft envelopes when required recipient data is present.
Checks envelope status and recipient progress for signing workflows.
Adds templates to draft envelopes when the workflow allows it.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active DocuSign connection before use.
Depends on current DocuSign tool schemas returned by Rube MCP.
Cannot bypass DocuSign account permissions, OAuth, or rate limits.
Does not create custom document content outside available DocuSign tools.

use_cases.title: Send Template-Based Agreements
use_cases.description: Prepare and send a DocuSign envelope from an existing template with mapped signer roles.

use_cases.title: Track Signature Progress
use_cases.description: Check envelope and recipient status so teams can follow up on pending signatures.

use_cases.title: Maintain Draft Envelopes
use_cases.description: Review draft envelopes and add templates before sending documents for signature.

prompt_templates.title: List Available Templates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the DocuSign automation skill to search current Rube MCP tool schemas, then list available DocuSign templates I can use.

prompt_templates.title: Create Draft Envelope
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the template named [template name], review its required roles, and create a draft envelope for [recipient names and emails].

prompt_templates.title: Send Prepared Envelope
prompt_templates.prompt: Check envelope [envelope ID], confirm it is ready to send, then send it only if all required recipients are present.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Signing Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Review envelope [envelope ID], summarize overall status, identify each recipient status, and recommend the next operational step.

output_examples.output: A concise list of matching templates with names, descriptions, role requirements, and template identifiers.

output_examples.input: Create a draft envelope from the onboarding template.
output_examples.output: A draft envelope summary showing the selected template, assigned recipients, email subject, and the envelope identifier.
A status report with the envelope state, recipient progress, sent time, completion time if available, and follow-up guidance.

best_practices: Search current Rube MCP tool schemas before each workflow.
Create drafts first when recipient data or template roles need review.
Use reasonable polling intervals when checking envelope status.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode template or envelope identifiers across DocuSign accounts.
Do not send envelopes before confirming required roles and email addresses.
Do not assume delivered means signed or completed.

faq.question: Does this skill require a DocuSign account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an active DocuSign connection through Rube MCP before running workflows.

faq.question: Can it send envelopes immediately?
faq.answer: Yes, when the selected tool supports sending and all required recipient details are present.

faq.question: Can it create envelopes from templates?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides template selection, role mapping, draft creation, and sending.

faq.question: Can it track signing progress?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks envelope status and recipient-level progress through DocuSign tools.

faq.question: Does it work without Rube MCP?
faq.answer: No. The skill is written for the Rube MCP DocuSign toolkit.

faq.question: Does it manage DocuSign permissions?
faq.answer: No. It follows the permissions and OAuth access already available in DocuSign.
